description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly off-white to beige coloration, interspersed with darker gray and brown mineral veins. The surface is rough and uneven, featuring jagged edges and natural fractures, with some areas appearing more weathered than others. One side of the stone displays a dark patch, possibly a mineral inclusion, while another side has a visible label or marking, suggesting it may have been cataloged or collected. The stone's texture is coarse, with visible striations and layering that hint at its geological formation. Its mass and density are consistent with common urban landscaping stones, and it appears solid throughout. The overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban environment.
description: A rough, irregular beige stone with dark mineral veins and a visible catalog label, featuring coarse texture and jagged edges.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's angled face with a prominent white-beige surface and a dark mineral patch near the top, as well as a visible catalog label.
description_view_1: From this angle, the stone's darker mineral inclusion is more pronounced, and the label is partially visible on the sloped surface.
description_view_2: This side shows the stone's elongated profile, with the dark mineral area extending along the upper edge and the label just visible at the corner.
description_view_3: The broadest face is visible here, showing layered striations, a rough surface, and the catalog label clearly displayed on the side.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or grouped with other stones along pathways or borders
attribute_pattern: Irregular veining and layering with mottled mineral inclusions, creating a natural, non-uniform pattern.
attribute_weathering: Rough and slightly weathered, with visible surface abrasions and minor discoloration indicating moderate exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured urban surfaces but can blend with natural landscaping elements or other stones in a designed setting.
